minimal_context: The student is working individually on math exercises on a computer in the lab, trying to achieve five correct answers in a row.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please continue working individually on your math exercises on the computer.

STUDENT: (Looking at the computer screen) I am trying to get five-in-a-row, but I just got this answer wrong. I'm going to try using the same strategy again for the next problem.

TUTOR: Keep trying your best.

STUDENT: I got it wrong again. I don't want to click the hint button because it will automatically give me an X.

TUTOR: What do you think you should do next?

STUDENT: I'm wary of using the embedded hints because it interrupts my accumulation of check marks and I'll have to rebuild back towards five-in-a-row. Can you just help me with it so I don't lose my points?

options.A: Deliver opportunities to respond consistently to the student during the computer math exercises.
options.B: Recognize and praise the student's appropriate behavior consistently during the computer math exercises.
options.C: Choose a few more advanced students in the lab to act as peer mentors for the math exercises.
options.D: Allow the student to work at their own pace and take a break from the computer as needed.
